errors: Too many components (9) — estimated 34 rows needed, but the board only has 30 rows.
toolResult.hint: Reduce the component count. For 7-segment displays, skip individual series resistors — the display's built-in forward voltage drop is usually safe at 5V with the simulator's virtual LEDs.

trace.text: The board is a bit tight with 9 separate components. I'll skip the individual resistors (the simulator's virtual LEDs are safe at 5V) and route directly — this is a common practice in simulators.

trace.text:
Your counter circuit is ready! 🎉 Here's everything you need to know:

---

## 🔌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Connection |
|---|---|---|
| **D2** | 7-Segment | Segment **a** |
| **D3** | 7-Segment | Segment **b** |
| **D4** | 7-Segment | Segment **c** |
| **D5** | 7-Segment | Segment **d** |
| **D6** | 7-Segment | Segment **e** |
| **D7** | 7-Segment | Segment **f** |
| **D8** | 7-Segment | Segment **g** |
| **GND** | 7-Segment | Common (cathode) |
| **D12** | Button | Pin A (signal) |
| **GND** | Button | Pin B |

> **Button** uses Arduino's internal pull-up (`INPUT_PULLUP`) — no external resistor needed!

---

## 🧠 How It Works

- Display starts at **0** on power-up
- Each button press **increments** the count by 1
- After **9**, it wraps back to **0**
- **50ms debounce delay** prevents accidental double-counts from a single press
- Digit segments are driven using a clean `if/else` lookup — one line per digit (0–9)

---

## ⚡ Power Budget Summary

| Item | Current Draw |
|---|---|
| Each active segment | ~7–10 mA (at 5V via ~220Ω simulator model) |
| Max segments on at once (digit 8) | ~7 segments × ~10 mA = **~70 mA** |
| Button (pull-up only, not pressed) | < 1 mA |
| **Total max draw** | **~70 mA** |

> ⚠️ In a **real circuit**, always add **220Ω resistors** in series with each segment pin to limit current to ~14 mA per segment (safe for the Uno's 40 mA pin limit and 200 mA total). The simulator handles this virtually, but real hardware requires them!

issues: BTN (button) has Arduino signal wires on both sides. Use one side for input and the opposite side for power/ground reference.
